results.title: Alternative Funds | Kairos
results.content:
European Long Term Investment Funds (ELTIF), established or managed by Kairos , are closed-end Alternative Investment Funds (AIF), available to both professional investors and retail investors who hold certain requirements with regards to knowledge and experience, financial situation and investment goals, pursuant to articles 28 and subsequent of Regulation (EU) 2015/760 of the European Parliament and Council of 29 April 2015. Given that it is closed-end, investments in ELTIFs should be considered illiquid as the investors do not have the right to redeem their units until the end of the life-cycle of the fund in question. [...] ##### SELECT COUNTRY

Alternative Investment Funds (AIF), established or managed by Kairos, are reserved for professional investors, as defined in CONSOB Regulation on Intermediaries. Certain AIF, if established in their documentation, may also be subscribed by non-professional investors for a total amount of no less than € 100,000, pursuant to Italian Ministerial Decree No. 30 of 5 March 2015, as modified by Ministerial Decree No. 19 of 13 January 2022. [...] In order to access information relating to AIFs and ELTIF Funds, it is necessary to click on “Confirm” to confirm that you understand the restrictions on accessing investment in the aforementioned funds and the illiquid nature of investment in ELTIF Funds.

results.title: Make Time Count by Understanding 2 Forms of Time: Kairos and Chronos
results.content:
When you interact and pay attention to others, you'll find yourself crossing from chronos to kairos ever more frequently. When you work with those who have many and deep needs, you are positioning yourself for these moments.

#### Chronos time management is obsessed with saving time. Kairos is about savoring it.

Simply put, we need both to be excellent. We must:

 Organize our lives to get the right things done on time,
 Know when it is the right time to put down the list and seize a moment that matters,
 Discern when people are wasting our time with pointless pursuits, and
 Recognize when a once in a moment opportunity has arisen to make a difference. [...] ## Kairos Time: Making Moments that Matter

However, there is more to time than endless lists, appointments, and to do's. Meet kairos. I’ve gone through periods in my life when I have scheduled every single moment. Because, as Michael Hyatt says, “what gets scheduled gets done.”

Undoubtedly, though, you can do every small thing and never do the one thing you were meant to do with your life or your day. Kairos is that moment. Kairos means

> “the right, critical, or opportune moment.”

In modern Greek it means

> “weather.”

### Timeless moments

Quite literally Kairos can be those once in a lifetime chances. Those things that you must stop and seize and smell and savor for they will be gone.

There’s something transcendent about kairos time. [...] ### How do we understand the two forms of time?

In ancient Greek, there are two words used for time: chronos and kairos. As we dig into these two concepts, we see two aspects of our time management to balance as work to be more excellent.

## Chronos Time: The Clock

So, chronos time is what you’re used to. According to the Greek, the name for sequential time. Named after the Greek god, Chronos, who was and three-headed being with the head of a man, a snake, and a lion — chronos is the ticking time we’re used to.

Chronos is a snake because it deceives you into devaluing its importance and a lion because when it runs out on you, it kills you. And chronos is a man because as Booker T Washington in his autobiography Up From Slavery says,

results.title: How to be prolific: the art of kairos time | Marty Neumeier posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
# How to be prolific: the art of kairos time

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

Marty Neumeier

 Report this post

The Greeks understood there were two types of time: CHRONOS, meaning objective time or clock time, and KAIROS, meaning subjective time. While chronos can be useful for ordinary time management, kairos is better for concentrating the mind on creative tasks. The world’s reservoir of time empties at the rate of 60 minutes per hour, 24 hours per day, 365 hours per year. While few of us say we have enough time, each of us has all the time we have. What most of us lack, especially in the creative class, are productivity skills. We kill at kairos, but we stink at chronos. More in "How to be prolific," at

results.title: 20 Best Productivity Tools: Hands-On Review
results.content:
Best fit for: People who need a personal task manager that syncs across all devices.

### #4: Notion — best for block-based notes

Notion is the most popular block-based note editor, making it one of the most widely used productivity apps in the world.

In Notion, everything you write or import exists as a block — a heading, a checklist, a text paragraph, an image, etc. I can move these building blocks around my document by simply holding and moving them with my cursor.

Adding a new block in Notion

Adding a new block in Notion [...] | Chanty |

| Free plan | Yes |
| Free trial | No |
| Cheapest paid plan | $3/user/month |
| Availability | Android, iOS, Web, MacOS, Windows, Linux |

Best fit for: Businesses with a solid budget that want collaboration and team communication in one tool.

### #3: Plaky — overall best project and task management app

Plaky is a free project management software that allows teams to collaborate on different projects and tasks.

I use Plaky daily, and the user interface is refreshingly simple for me, while still providing a powerful set of project management features. Like on a Kanban board, my colleagues and I create cards for each task, and fill in details like:

 Status,
 Assignees,
 Deadlines,
 Reporters,
 Priority, and more. [...] | Plaky |

| Free plan | Yes |
| Free trial | 14-day free trial |
| Cheapest paid plan | $3/user/month |
| Availability | Android, iOS, Web, MacOS, Windows, Linux |

Best fit for: Mid-sized and small businesses that need a simple yet powerful task management app.

### #4: IFTTT — best automation app for beginners

IFTTT, meaning If This Then That, is the simplest online automation service that connects various online services via a basic trigger-based causal relationship. It’s a great alternative to AI-based automations, which are often overwhelming for beginners and overkill for many use cases.

Here’s an example of a problem I solved with IFTTT. I often forget to check the weather before I leave my house in the morning, so I’m rarely prepared for bad weather.
